rulings.Case Title: LENDING TEAM MORTGAGE, INC. VS. TAK TSUI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 8, 2008, line 14. CROSS DEFENDANT WING CHAN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. Tsui alleges facts that reflect payments made to Chan that Tsui was not required to pay. The facts state claims for conversion, fraud and common count. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LENDING TEAM MORTGAGE, INC. VS. TAK TSUI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 8, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANTS TAK TSUI, TAK TSUI MORTGAGE, INC. AND GRACE LAW'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden of producing evidence because plaintiff failed to negate Chan's testimony as to when he learned of Tsui's and Law's conduct. (defendant's facts 5, 7, 9, 14). The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the 7th cause of action because unjust enrichment is a remedy not a cause of action. It is denied as to the other causes of action. The burden did not shift as the 1st, 2nd, 4th, 5th and 8th causes of action. (defendant's facts 5, 7, 9, 14, 24, 25, 26, 33, 34, 35). There are triable issues of fact as to the third and sixth causes of action (defendant's facts 5, 7, 9, 14, 19, 20, 24, 25, 26, 40; plaintiff's facts 2, 3, 5). =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RUTH ELOISE LEPERA et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 8, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifts the burden of producing evidence on the issue of actual or constructive notice. Plaintiff presents evidence that raises a triable issue of fact on the issue of notice. See plaintiff's response to defendants' undisputed material facts 11 and plaintiffs' additional facts 8-10. Plaintiffs' objection to evidence submitted in the reply is sustained. Plaintiffs' objections to evidence in the moving papers are overruled except for 5 as to the text after the word "and." =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MAGGY SABET BREWER et al VS. DISRTIRUBTECH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 8, 2008, line 16, PLAINTIFF MAGGY BREWER'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT THE FOLLOWING ARE NOT AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES: 1, 11, 13, 15, 16, 19, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26 AND 28. DEFENSE 10 APPEARS TO ALLEGE 3 DISTINCT AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. =(302/PJM/PB)
